content:
# Nested Loops

An inner loop within the repeating block of an outer loop is called Nested Loop.

The **Inner Loop** will be executed one time for each iteration of the **Outer Loop.**


![](https://res.cloudinary.com/learning-platform/image/fetch/dpr_auto,w_auto,f_auto,q_auto/https://nkb-backend-ccbp-media-static.s3.ap-south-1.amazonaws.com/prod/media/profile_pic/card-notes-attachment/8eb1b96f-d188-4ef4-8d29-f270a945b489.png)
  

#### Code

```python
for i in range(2):
  print("Outer: " + str(i))
  for j in range(2):
    print(" Inner: " + str(j))
```

#### Output

```
Outer: 0
 Inner: 0
 Inner: 1
Outer: 1
 Inner: 0
 Inner: 1
 ```

## Nested Repeating Block

The one highlighted in the blue dotted line is the **repeating** block of the inner loop.

  
![](https://res.cloudinary.com/learning-platform/image/fetch/dpr_auto,w_auto,f_auto,q_auto/https://nkb-backend-ccbp-media-static.s3.ap-south-1.amazonaws.com/prod/media/profile_pic/card-notes-attachment/f5d32b60-693e-4b2a-b65a-3b15c546cf5c.png)

#### Code

```python
for i in range(2):
  print("Outer: " + str(i))
  for j in range(2):
    print(" Inner: " + str(j))
print("END")
```

In the above example, the below line is the repeating block of the nested loop.

#### Code
```python
print(" Inner: " + str(j))
```

#### Output

```
Outer: 0
 Inner: 0
 Inner: 1
Outer: 1
 Inner: 0
 Inner: 1
END
```

## Examples - Nested Loops

**Example - 1**: While loop inside a For loop

![](https://res.cloudinary.com/learning-platform/image/fetch/dpr_auto,w_auto,f_auto,q_auto/https://nkb-backend-ccbp-media-static.s3.ap-south-1.amazonaws.com/prod/media/profile_pic/card-notes-attachment/00845cd4-e8ca-4f52-b729-13de7fc86a72.png)

**Example - 2**: While loop inside a while loop


![](https://res.cloudinary.com/learning-platform/image/fetch/dpr_auto,w_auto,f_auto,q_auto/https://nkb-backend-ccbp-media-static.s3.ap-south-1.amazonaws.com/prod/media/profile_pic/card-notes-attachment/b5bbe75b-d0f6-4dd6-ad18-dc7fea759518.png)
